title: circaea lutetiana proles intermedia
intro: Circaea lutetiana proles intermedia, also known as the Intermediate Wood Sorrel, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Gentianaceae. It is characterized by its heart-shaped leaves and red or pink flowers. The plant has been used in traditional herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are said to aid in the treatment of wounds, skin irritations, and infections. Herbal preparations of Circaea lutetiana proles intermedia include infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be used to promote wound healing, reduce inflammation, and soothe skin irritations.
